=== THE LAW & THE ANTI-OVERFIT PRINCIPLE ===
An ATTRIBUTED clause must be ENTAILED by its source span. DELETION is not truth-preserving: dropping a
direction / contrast / negation / scope / hedge / comparator / temporal / condition / quantifier word can
flip the claim. The space of meaning-changing words is OPEN -- you cannot enumerate it. Every fix that
LISTED the dangerous words was defeated by a word not on the list (plunged, -3, trailing scope, 'the data
suggest that'...). THE GENERAL RULE MUST NOT ENUMERATE DANGEROUS WORDS.

THE ZERO-DROP RULE: the deterministic layer admits WITHOUT the judge ONLY when the attributed clause drops
NOTHING -- it is, after whitespace/case/punctuation normalization, IDENTICAL to a complete sentence or a
complete top-level clause of the span (zero words shed at either edge relative to that clause). ANY
truncation -- a proper substring of a source clause -- MIGHT have dropped a meaning-changer, so it goes to
the JUDGE and FAILS CLOSED (unreachable/UNCERTAIN -> REJECT). A false reject is safe; a false admit is
catastrophic.


YOUR TASK: replace the overfit poison-list entailment admit in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py with the
GENERAL ZERO-DROP RULE. The current contiguous_window_admit relies on _LEAD_POISON/_TRAIL_POISON/_UP/_DOWN/
_HEDGE word lists; a fresh adversary broke it on scope (#10a) and hedge (#10b) and predicts more
(comparator/temporal/condition/quantifier). STOP ENUMERATING.

IMPLEMENT:
 1. The deterministic ADMIT fires ONLY when the normalized attributed clause is IDENTICAL to a complete
    source unit -- a full sentence, or a full top-level clause of the span. "Drops nothing at either edge."
    Normalization: whitespace collapse, case fold, strip surrounding punctuation. NO word lists decide
    admit.
 2. If the attributed clause is a PROPER SUBSTRING of any source unit (i.e. it is contiguous but sheds one
    or more words at an edge), DO NOT admit deterministically -> route to the judge -> fail closed. It does
    not matter WHICH words were shed; we do not judge that deterministically anymore.
 3. Keep the REJECT-only deterministic pre-filter (a number/entity/venue in the clause not in the span ->
    reject) -- those are sound rejects and reduce judge load. But NOTHING deterministic may ADMIT except
    zero-drop identity.
 4. CLAUSE-BOUNDARY DETECTION: to find "a complete top-level clause" you may split on strong punctuation
    (. ; : and sentence end) and coordinating/subordinating boundaries. BUT if clause-splitting itself
    would need an open word list, PREFER THE SAFE UNDER-SPLIT: split on LESS, so a "clause" is larger, so
    fewer things count as whole-clause-identical, so MORE goes to the judge. Under-splitting fails safe
    (more judge, never more admit). Over-splitting (treating a fragment as a whole clause) is the hole --
    avoid it. When unsure whether a boundary exists, DO NOT split there.
 5. Retire the now-dead _UP/_DOWN/_LEAD_POISON/_TRAIL_POISON/_HEDGE/_modality_residue machinery from the
    admit path (leave any that other lanes still use, but the ADMIT decision must not consult them).

VERIFY yourself, judge DOWN and judge=NOT_ENTAILED (identical results required):
  * Every truncation REJECTS (deterministic admit does not fire): #9 subsequence, #10a scope
    ('boosted employment in the adopting cities'->'boosted employment'), #10b hedge ('the data suggest
    that wages grew'->'wages grew'), comparator ('rose more than expected'->'rose'), temporal
    ('rose in 2020'->'rose'), condition ('rose when demand is elastic'->'rose'), quantifier
    ('some firms cut jobs'->'firms cut jobs'). ALL reject with the judge down.
  * A ZERO-DROP true finding SHIPS with judge down (judge_calls=0): the attributed clause equals a whole
    source clause verbatim.
  * All 8 burns still REJECT; test_gate_is_wired + test_fabrication_paths GREEN; git diff shows NO test
    weakened (do not swap a positive control to make it pass -- that trick has appeared before).
Report file:line, the general rule as implemented, and real output for every case above. Do NOT commit.

941	        else:

943	            regions.append(rid)
944	    return words, regions
945	
946	
947	def contiguous_window_admit(text: str, span: str, min_words: int = 4) -> bool:
948	    """THE ONLY DETERMINISTIC ADMIT. True iff `text` is a contiguous word-window of ONE clause of `span`
949	    that sheds no meaning-flipping term at either edge. It NEVER admits a subsequence, a window that
950	    crosses a strong break, or a window that drops a leading negation/scope/direction/contrast — those
951	    fall through to the judge. See the block comment above for why this is truth-preserving."""
952	    cw, _ = _words_regions(text)
953	    if len(cw) < min_words:
954	        return False
955	    sw, sr = _words_regions(span)
956	    n = len(cw)
957	    for i in range(len(sw) - n + 1):
958	        if sw[i:i + n] != cw:
959	            continue
960	        if sr[i] != sr[i + n - 1]:
961	            continue                                   # the window crosses a strong '.'/';'/':' break
962	        region = sr[i]
963	        lead = [sw[k] for k in range(i) if sr[k] == region]
964	        trail = [sw[k] for k in range(i + n, len(sw)) if sr[k] == region]
965	        if any(w in _LEAD_POISON for w in lead):
966	            continue
967	        if any(w in _TRAIL_POISON for w in trail):
968	            continue
969	        return True
970	    return False
971	
972	
973	def entailed_by_span(text: str, span: str, work: P.Work | None = None,
974	                     min_overlap: float = 0.25) -> tuple[bool, str]:
975	    """Is THIS text ENTAILED by THIS verbatim span? The span is the only evidence there is.
976	
977	    `claim` — the model's restatement — is not a parameter of this function and never will be. The old
978	    gate read `src = f'{span} {claim}'`, and since the writer was handed only the claim, the chain was:
979	    the model writes the claim -> the writer writes from the claim -> the gate checks the writing
980	    against the claim. THE GATE VALIDATED THE MODEL AGAINST ITSELF.
981	
982	    Three layers, in order:
983	
984	      1. A DETERMINISTIC PRE-FILTER that may only REJECT (numbers+units, a strict direction opposition, a
985	         content floor). It is cheap and certain and it kills the obvious fabrication offline. Surviving
986	         it means "not yet rejected", never "entailed".
987	      2. THE ONE SOUND DETERMINISTIC ADMIT: a NON-numeric clause that is a contiguous window of ONE
988	         source clause, dropping no meaning-flipping edge qualifier (`contiguous_window_admit`). This is
989	         the only deletion a span cannot lie by, so it admits even with the judge down — the true finding
990	         reaches the page during a model blip instead of being starved. Everything else falls through.
991	      3. THE JUDGE, consulted on the WHOLE clause vs the WHOLE span for EVERY clause that survives (1) and
992	         is not admitted by (2), not conditioned on any word list. ONLY 'ENTAILED' admits; 'NOT_ENTAILED'
993	         and 'UNCERTAIN' — and a judge that cannot be reached — REJECT. This is the line that catches the
994	         synonym sign-flip ('plunged'), the magnitude fabrication ('doubled'), the scope swap
995	         ('worldwide') and the modality flip ('causes'), none of which any lexicon here names.
996	    """
997	    src = (span or '').lower()
998	    if not src:
999	        return False, 'NO_SPAN'
1000	    ctext = (text or '').lower()
1001	
1002	    # ============ DETERMINISTIC PRE-FILTER — REJECT-ONLY. It may kill; it may never admit. ============
1003	    # (A) NUMBERS + UNITS. Every quantity in the clause must stand alone in the span with the SAME unit.
1004	    #     No single-digit exemption. No year exemption. "1.5 points" does not support "1.5 percent".
1005	    span_q = quantities_in(src)
1006	    for num, unit in quantities_in(ctext):
1007	        if not _quantity_supported(num, unit, src, span_q):
1008	            return False, f'NUMBER_OR_UNIT_NOT_IN_SPAN:{num}{(" " + unit) if unit else ""}'
1009	    # A bare integer with no recognised unit (e.g. a fabricated "2021 regions") is still a quantity.
1010	    for num in numbers_in(ctext):
1011	        if not number_stands_alone(num, src):
1012	            return False, f'NUMBER_NOT_IN_SPAN:{num}'
1013	
1014	    # (B) DIRECTION / POLARITY. Reject only a strict opposition — clause one way, span the other way.
1015	    #     This catches the IN-lexicon flip ("fell" vs "rose") without a model call; the OUT-of-lexicon
1016	    #     flip ("plunged", "slid", "weakened") is left for the judge, which is the whole point of (2).
1017	    cw = set(re.findall(r'[a-z]+', ctext))
1018	    sw = set(re.findall(r'[a-z]+', src))
1019	    c_up, c_down = bool(cw & _UP), bool(cw & _DOWN)
1020	    s_up, s_down = bool(sw & _UP), bool(sw & _DOWN)
1021	    if (c_down and not c_up and s_up and not s_down) or (c_up and not c_down and s_down and not s_up):
1022	        return False, 'DIRECTION_CONTRADICTS_SPAN'
1023	
1024	    # (C) CONTENT FLOOR. A clause whose content words are largely absent from the span is unsupported.
1025	    words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ctext)} - _STOP
1026	    if work:
1027	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', (work.venue or '').lower())}
1028	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ' '.join(work.authors or []).lower())}
1029	    src_words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', src)}
1030	    if words and len(words & src_words) / len(words) < min_overlap:
1031	        return False, 'CONTENT_NOT_IN_SPAN'
1032	
1033	    # ============ THE ONE SOUND DETERMINISTIC ADMIT — a contiguous window of ONE source clause =========
1034	    # This is the ONLY place the deterministic layer concludes ADMIT, and it is truth-preserving: a
1035	    # contiguous window of one clause that sheds no edge qualifier cannot flip meaning by deletion (see
1036	    # `contiguous_window_admit`). It lets a verbatim NON-numeric finding reach the page even when the
1037	    # judge is unreachable, so a real compose is not emptied by a blip. A clause carrying a FIGURE is
1038	    # excluded — numbers stay on the judge and are held when it is down (the fail-closed guarantee for
1039	    # sign/magnitude is not traded away). Everything else falls through to the judge below.
1040	    if not re.search(r'\d', ctext) and not _SPELLED_UNIT.search(ctext) \
1041	            and contiguous_window_admit(text, span):
1042	        return True, ''
1043	
1044	    # ============ THE JUDGE — WHOLE CLAUSE vs WHOLE SPAN, EVERY TIME. ONLY 'ENTAILED' ADMITS. ==========
1045	    verdict, why = _semantic_judge(text, span)
1046	    if verdict != _ENTAILED:
1047	        return False, f'JUDGE_{verdict}' + (f' :: {why}' if why else '')
1048	    return True, ''
1049	
1050	
1051	# =================================================================================================
1052	# 4. VALIDATION — EVERY NODE, EVERY TIME, AT RENDER AND AGAIN AT PUBLISH
1053	# =================================================================================================
1054	
1055	MARKER = re.compile(r'\[\d+\]')
1056	PAREN_YEAR = re.compile(r'\((?:19|20)\d\d[a-z]?\)')
1057	BANNED_META = re.compile(
1058	    r'\b(this report|this review synthesi[sz]es|the pipeline|retrieved|the question above|'
1059	    r'span-grounded|telemetry|our system|we retrieved|the corpus|evidence card)\b', re.I)

221	def t2b_owned_number(b):

223	    admitted, refusal = _admitted([n], b)

227	# ---- T3  HEADING: an untyped, unreceipted heading that ASSERTS a fabricated finding -----------------
228	def t3_heading(b):

230	    admitted, refusal = _admitted([n], b)
231	    # ALSO show it reaches the page: render() emits it verbatim.
232	    reached = ''
233	    try:
234	        md, _ = A.render([n], b)
235	        reached = md.strip()
236	    except Exception as e:                                                # noqa: BLE001
237	        reached = f'(render refused: {e})'
238	    record('T3  heading   ("Acemoglu proves that 47 percent...")', admitted, refusal,
239	           note=f'render() emitted -> {reached!r}')

241	
242	# ---- T4  TABLE: a row whose model `claim` REVERSES its span, with invented level/method -------------
243	def t4_table(b):
244	    n = EvidenceTable(card_ids=('c:up_rev', 'c:bres', 'c:autor'))
245	    admitted, refusal = _admitted([n], b)
246	    note = ''
247	    try:
248	        md, _ = A.render([n], b)
249	        note = 'table row shipped: ' + ('fell by 1.5 points' in md
250	                                        and 'children with cancer' in md and 'randomized trial' in md
251	                                        and 'REVERSED claim + invented level/method printed' or md[:80])
252	    except Exception as e:                                                # noqa: BLE001
253	        note = f'(render refused: {e})'
254	    record('T4  table     (claim REVERSES span; invented level/method)', admitted, refusal, note=note)
255	
256	
257	# ---- T5  CONNECTIVE: two positive, unrelated findings joined by "by contrast" -----------------------
258	def t5_connective(b):
259	    n = Attributed(clauses=(
260	        Clause('c:bres', 'computer automation of such work has been correspondingly limited '
261	                         'in its scope'),
262	        Clause('c:autor', 'computer capital substitutes for workers in carrying out a limited and '
263	                          'well-defined set of cognitive and manual activities, namely routine tasks'),
264	    ), connective='by contrast')

266	    record('T5  connective (two POSITIVE findings joined by "by contrast")', admitted, refusal)

268	
269	# ---- T6  VENUE: the model types a journal name ("Science") under an AER card ------------------------
270	def t6_venue(b):
271	    n = Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:up',
272	        'Science reports that the local employment-to-population ratio rose by 1.5 points in regions '
273	        'that adopted the technology'),))

275	    note = ''
276	    try:
277	        md, _ = A.render([n], b)
278	        note = f'rendered under AER card -> {md.strip()[:120]!r}'
279	    except Exception as e:                                                # noqa: BLE001
280	        note = f'(render refused: {e})'
281	    record('T6  venue     (model types "Science" under an AER card)', admitted, refusal, note=note)
282	
283	
284	# ---- T7  OWNED VERDICT: CONVERGES admits BOTH "opposite directions" AND "not contradictory" ---------
285	def t7_owned_verdict(b):
286	    opposed = Owned(
287	        text='These employment findings across regions point in opposite directions.',
288	        premise_ids=('c:up', 'c:down'))
289	    reconciled = Owned(
290	        text='These employment findings across regions are not contradictory.',
291	        premise_ids=('c:up', 'c:down'))
292	    a1, r1 = _admitted([opposed], b)
293	    a2, r2 = _admitted([reconciled], b)
294	    both = a1 and a2
295	    # At most ONE of two contradictory reconciliations may pass. If BOTH pass, a false reconciliation
296	    # is admissible -> the hole is open.
297	    record('T7  owned-verdict (BOTH "opposite directions" AND "not contradictory" admitted)',
298	           both,
299	           f'opposite-directions: {"ADMIT" if a1 else "REJECT:" + r1} | '
300	           f'not-contradictory: {"ADMIT" if a2 else "REJECT:" + r2}',
301	           note='at most one may pass; both passing = false reconciliation is admissible')
302	
303	
304	def main() -> int:
305	    b = build_bundle()
306	    for t in (p0_positive_control, p1_positive_synthesis, p2_positive_heading,
307	              t1_negation, t1b_units, t1c_single_digit, t1d_fake_year,
308	              t2_owned_fact, t2b_owned_number, t3_heading, t4_table,
309	              t5_connective, t6_venue, t7_owned_verdict):
310	        t(b)
311	
312	    print('=== SOL LADDER RUNG 2+3a — FABRICATION TESTS (driving the REAL validator) ===')
313	    print('    Each attack MUST be REJECTED. The P0/P1/P2 POSITIVE CONTROLS MUST be ADMITTED.\n')
314	    # Rung 2 closes the ENTAILMENT lanes: T1 (direction), T1b (units), T1c (single digit), T1d (year),
315	    # and T4 (the evidence-table row, which shares this same entailment check).
316	    # Rung 3a closes the OWNED-FRAME and HEADING lanes: T2 (premise-free owned fact), T2b (premise-free
317	    # owned spelled quantity), and T3 (a heading that asserts a fabricated finding).
318	    # Rung 4 closes the VERDICT lane: T7 (a false reconciliation — "not contradictory" over premises that
319	    # share a scope). An owned verdict now carries the operation its own claim names and ships ONLY if
320	    # that operation's proof holds against span-bound facets. At most ONE of two contradictory
321	    # reconciliations may pass; both passing is the hole, and it is now closed.
322	    RUNG2 = {'T1 ', 'T1b', 'T1c', 'T1d', 'T4 '}
323	    RUNG3 = {'T2 ', 'T2b', 'T3 '}
324	    RUNG4 = {'T7'}
325	
326	    def tag(name):  # the short test id at the head of the name
327	        return name.split('(')[0].strip()[:3].rstrip()
328	
329	    holes_open = 0
330	    positive_regressed = False
331	    for name, admitted, refusal, note in RESULTS:
332	        is_positive = name[:2] in ('P0', 'P1', 'P2')
333	        if is_positive:
334	            if admitted:
335	                print(f'  [OK  — legitimate node SHIPPED] {name}')
336	            else:
337	                positive_regressed = True
338	                print(f'  [REGRESSION — LEGITIMATE REJECTED] {name}')
339	                print(f'                                 refusal: {refusal}')
340	            continue
341	        if admitted:
342	            holes_open += 1
343	            print(f'  [HOLE OPEN  — attack ADMITTED] {name}')
344	            if note:
345	                print(f'                                 {note}')
346	        else:
347	            print(f'  [closed — attack REJECTED    ] {name}')
348	            print(f'                                 refusal: {refusal}')
349	
350	    def _open(rung):
351	        ids = {t.strip() for t in rung}
352	        return [name for name, admitted, _, _ in RESULTS
353	                if admitted and name[:2] not in ('P0', 'P1', 'P2') and tag(name) in ids]
354	
355	    rung2_open, rung3_open, rung4_open = _open(RUNG2), _open(RUNG3), _open(RUNG4)
356	    print(f'\n  Rung-2 lanes still open (entailment): {len(rung2_open)}  (target: 0)')
357	    print(f'  Rung-3a lanes still open (owned/heading): {len(rung3_open)}  (target: 0)')
358	    print(f'  Rung-4 lanes still open (false verdict): {len(rung4_open)}  (target: 0)')
359	    print(f'  Positive controls: {"REGRESSED — a legitimate node was rejected" if positive_regressed else "OK — every legitimate node still ships"}')
360	    ok = (not rung2_open) and (not rung3_open) and (not rung4_open) and (not positive_regressed)
361	    if ok:
362	        print('  RUNG 2+3a+4 CLEAR: every entailment/owned/heading/verdict attack is rejected AND every '
363	              'legitimate node still ships.')
364	    else:
365	        print('  NOT CLEAR: a lane is open or a positive control regressed.')
366	    return 0 if ok else 1
367	
368	
369	if __name__ == '__main__':
370	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3

4	WHY THIS EXISTS, IN ONE PARAGRAPH:
5	I built `synthesis_contract.py`, ran 14 adversarial attacks I had written myself, watched it print "ZERO
6	FALSE ADMISSIONS", and reported it as working. An adversarial reviewer then found that `validate()` was
7	imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and **never called anywhere in the repo except its own self_test()**.
8	The gate was a closed loop: invoked only by its own test, fed its own examples, printing green.
9	
10	WHY IT WAS REWRITTEN — AND THIS IS THE WHOLE LESSON OF THE NIGHT:
11	This canary then went 16/16 GREEN while SIX ADVERSARY ATTACKS SUCCEEDED and nothing had been weakened.
12	It was not defeated. It was ORPHANED. It drove `cellcog_composer._clean()` with hand-built dicts —
13	`{'authors': [...], 'span': '...'}` — and the fabrication had moved to a lane where cards carry
14	manifestations and content hashes. **THE CHECKS CERTIFIED A LANE THE FABRICATION NO LONGER USED.**
15	
16	A canary that tests a dead function is worse than no canary, because it is green.
17	
18	So every fixture here is now a BOUND card in a real graph (`_test_fixtures.py`), and every check drives
19	THE CODE THAT ACTUALLY SHIPS: `report_ast.validate_report()` -> `publisher.publish()` -> the sealed
20	release directory. If the composer, the AST or the publisher stops calling the gate, this goes red.
21	
22	    python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py

24	from __future__ import annotations

26	import ast
27	import inspect
28	import json
29	import re
30	import sys
31	from pathlib import Path
32	
33	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
